robot.clear: Turn off a part of the face.
robot.initialize: Initialize the robot face on the given data pin. Connect the NeoPixel to Servo1 on the servo board: Servo1 is wired to pin P8.
robot.mirrorToScreen: Also mirror the expression on the micro:bit's 5x5 LED screen.
robot.mirrorToArms: Also move the arms (servo2) to match the expression.
robot.turnServoToPosition: Turn a servo to a position (high, middle or low).
robot.turnServoToValue: Turn a servo to a value (angle) between 0 and 180 degrees.
robot.turnServoWithKnob: Turn a servo to an angle (0-180) from a knob (potentiometer) connected to an analog pin.
robot.reverseServo: Reverse the turning direction of a servo. When on, an angle of 180 becomes 0, 0 becomes 180, 30 becomes 150, and so on.
robot.limitServoRange: Limit the range of a servo so it can only move between a fixed start and end position. The start must be smaller than the end.
robot.setBrightness: Set the overall brightness of the robot face (0-255).
robot.setColor: Set the color of a part of the face.
robot.showExpression: Show an expression on the robot face.
